lede: A look at May 10 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ivan Cankar and Denis Thatcher, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Syrian civil war
events.desc: Syrian civil war: The Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) capture the last footholds of the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) in Al-Tabqah, bringing the Battle of Tabqa to an end.

events.title: One World Trade Center
events.desc: One World Trade Center becomes the tallest building in the Western Hemisphere.

events.title: Mount Everest
events.desc: A blizzard strikes Mount Everest, killing eight climbers by the next day.

events.title: Thailand
events.desc: In Thailand, a fire at the Kader Toy Factory kills over 200 workers.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: German fighters accidentally bomb the German city of Freiburg.

events.title: Censorship
events.desc: Censorship: In Germany, the Nazis stage massive public book burnings.

events.title: Kentucky
events.desc: American Civil War: In Kentucky, Union soldiers ambush and mortally wound Confederate raider William Quantrill, who lingers until his death on June 6.

events.title: First Barbary War
events.desc: First Barbary War: The Barbary pirates of Tripoli declare war on the United States of America.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: A small Colonial militia led by Ethan Allen and Colonel Benedict Arnold captures Fort Ticonderoga.

births.name: Denis Thatcher
births.line: English businessman, Spouse of the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom

deaths.text: Joan Crawford, American actress (year of birth disputed)
Mikhail Larionov, Russian painter, illustrator, and set designer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Alphius, Philadelphus and Cyrinus
